title: Enforce HIGH-risk gate in CI (expertSignOff + numeric verification + disclaimer required)
context: The HIGH-risk CI gate is the automated enforcement backstop for the HIGH-risk content rules established in arch-002 and encoded in the content schema (data-003). Without this gate, a HIGH-risk unit could be merged without expert sign-off or with unverified numeric claims. This gate is critical for content integrity and must run before any HIGH-risk unit (content-015, content-016, content-017) is merged.
objective: Implement and wire up a CI gate that fails the build for any content unit with riskClass:high that lacks a recorded expertSignOff, has any numericClaim without verifiedBy or with expertConfirmed:false, or is missing the required disclaimer and danger-signs fields; and passes for a well-formed HIGH-risk unit and all medium-risk units.
acceptanceCriteria: Build fails any riskClass:high unit that is missing the expertSignOff field (regardless of whether all other fields are valid)
Build fails any riskClass:high unit that has any numericClaim with missing verifiedBy, or with expertConfirmed not set to true
Build fails any riskClass:high unit that is missing the required disclaimer field or (for clinical units) the danger-signs/when-to-seek-care field
Build passes a well-formed HIGH-risk unit with expertSignOff, all numericClaims verified and expertConfirmed:true, and required disclaimer and danger-sign fields present
Build passes all medium-risk units regardless of expertSignOff (which is not required for medium risk)
Gate is integrated into the CI workflow (pnpm test or dedicated CI step); pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint all pass; reviewed and approved by a maintainer distinct from the author
output: A PR adding a CI gate that enforces the HIGH-risk content requirements (expertSignOff, numeric verification, disclaimer) with test cases covering both pass and fail scenarios.
